  Per ADR-042 element 7 + pheno-llms-txt (T20.7) v8 template (5-line quickstart + non-scope + 3 examples).
  Cross-references SPEC.md (T20.2), WORKLOG.md (T20.5), CHANGELOG.md (T20.6).
-->

# pheno-go-ctxkit

> Canonical context utilities for the Pheno Go HTTP-service fleet. Standardises three values every Pheno handler needs: a per-request UUID v4 id (header `X-Request-ID`), a `*slog.Logger` reachable from `context.Context`, and a `net/http` middleware that wires both together and emits a structured `request.complete` log line on the way out. Depends only on the Go standard library (`log/slog`, `crypto/rand`); no third-party deps.

## Quickstart (5 lines)

```go
import "github.com/kooshapari/pheno-go-ctxkit/ctxkit"
func main() {
    mux := http.NewServeMux()
    mux.HandleFunc("/ping", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        ctxkit.Logger(r.Context()).Info("ping", "id", ctxkit.RequestID(r.Context()))
        w.WriteHeader(204)
    })
    http.ListenAndServe(":8080", ctxkit.Middleware(mux))
}
```

## When to use

- You need a per-request id + `*slog.Logger` pair propagated through `context.Context` in a `net/http` handler chain.
- You want a single canonical `X-Request-ID` header for inbound + outbound requests.
- You want a `request.complete` access-log line emitted via the stdlib `log/slog` (no third-party logger).

## When NOT to use

- You need gRPC interceptors — `pheno-go-ctxkit` is `net/http` only; port the pattern manually.
- You need distributed-tracing context propagation (W3C `traceparent`) — use `go.opentelemetry.io/otel/propagation` alongside; this package only carries the request id.
- You need a third-party logger (zap / zerolog) — keep `log/slog`; the package only exposes `*slog.Logger`.

## 3 examples (illustrative)

1. **Inbound + outbound header round-trip** — `ctxkit_test.go` `TestMiddleware_PreservesExistingRequestID` verifies that an inbound request carrying `X-Request-ID` keeps its id, and `TestMiddleware_GeneratesUUIDWhenMissing` confirms a fresh UUID v4 is generated otherwise.
2. **`Logger` extraction with `slog.Default()` fallback** — `ctxkit.go` `Logger` returns the per-request logger if set, otherwise `slog.Default()`. Handlers can call `ctxkit.Logger(ctx).Info(...)` unconditionally.
3. **`WithRequestID` programmatic override** — middleware code paths can call `ctxkit.WithRequestID(ctx, "trace-123")` to inject a known id (e.g. when crossing an internal queue); the override is preserved through `RequestID` lookups.

## Documentation

- [AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md): project overview, conventions, governance for AI agents. Per-repo template (v8); cites ADR-023, ADR-042.
- [SPEC.md](SPEC.md): canonical 1-page specification (what / why / how / interface / status; per ADR-042 element 1). Cites the 3-value standardisation (id, logger, middleware).
- [WORKLOG.md](WORKLOG.md): change history (ADR-025 v2.1 schema, 11 columns including `device:` field). Validator: `pheno-worklog-schema` v2.1.
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): release notes, semver, breaking changes. Keep a Changelog 1.1.0.
- [STATUS.md](STATUS.md): weekly-refresh status doc with honest 71-pillar scorecard (Tier 0) and Factory AI Agent Readiness level 0 (Functional). Per ADR-041 + ADR-026.
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md): how to contribute. Branch prefixes, Conventional Commits, PR template.
- [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): Contributor Covenant 2.1.
- [SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md): vulnerability disclosure policy.

## Optional

- [go.mod](go.mod): module manifest (`github.com/kooshapari/pheno-go-ctxkit`, go 1.22). Zero third-party deps.
- [ctxkit/ctxkit.go](ctxkit/ctxkit.go): `WithRequestID`, `RequestID`, `NewRequestID`, `WithLogger`, `Logger`, `Middleware`, `HeaderRequestID`.
- [ctxkit/ctxkit_test.go](ctxkit/ctxkit_test.go): table-driven tests for inbound header preservation, UUID generation, logger extraction, middleware ordering.

## Sibling substrates (fleet)

- `pheno-context` (Rust) — request-context propagation; the Go `X-Request-ID` header is the wire-level mirror of the Rust `Context::request_id` field.
- `pheno-fastapi-base` (Python) — FastAPI base; uses structlog in lieu of `log/slog` but mirrors the same `X-Request-ID` convention.
- `phenotype-go-sdk` — polyglot substrate SDK; Go consumers reach this package through the SDK's `ctxkit` re-export.
- `pheno-otel` (Rust) — OTLP exporter; the Go `request.complete` log line is shipped via the Go-side OTLP exporter (not yet in-tree; tracked as a follow-up).

## ADRs (governance)

- [ADR-023 (Agent-effort governance)](../../docs/adr/2026-06-15/ADR-023-agent-effort-governance.md): substrate placement + Rule 3.1 quality bar.
- [ADR-037 (pheno-mcp-router substrate canonical)](../../docs/adr/2026-06-18/ADR-037-pheno-mcp-router-substrate-canonical.md): analogous substrate-assignment ADR.
- [ADR-038 (Hexagonal L4 Port/Adapter policy)](../../docs/adr/2026-06-18/ADR-038-hexagonal-port-adapter-l4-policy.md): pattern contract.
- [ADR-042 (Substrate quality bar)](../../docs/adr/2026-06-18/ADR-042-substrate-quality-bar.md): 7-element quality bar.
- [ADR-040 (Test coverage gates per tier)](../../docs/adr/2026-06-18/ADR-040-test-coverage-gates-per-tier.md): 80% lib/SDK gate.
- [ADR-025 (worklog v2.1 schema bump)](../../docs/adr/2026-06-17/ADR-025-adr-015-v2-1.md): `device:` field.
- [ADR-030 (pheno-worklog-schema v2.1)](../../docs/adr/2026-06-17/ADR-030-pheno-worklog-schema-v2-1.md): 4 `device:` values pinned.
- [ADR-041 (71-pillar refresh cadence)](../../docs/adr/2026-06-18/ADR-041-71-pillar-refresh-cadence.md): weekly probe cadence.
- [ADR-026 (Factory AI Agent Readiness)](../../docs/adr/2026-06-17/ADR-026-factory-ai-agent-readiness.md): external cross-cutting standard.

## See also

- [findings/71-pillar-2026-06-17.md § 1.x](../../findings/71-pillar-2026-06-17.md): 71-pillar scorecard (this crate scored Tier 0).
